summary: Validate and harden accessibility core flows
acceptance_criteria: Core Alpha flows are keyboard reachable, focus-visible, screen-reader labelable, and usable without color-only state for scenario start, command preview/confirm, map navigation, diplomacy, succession, policy/event choices, encyclopedia, save/load checkpoint, and victory/failure review.
Accessibility work uses existing UI patterns and minimal dependencies; any new production dependency includes purpose, maintenance, license, alternatives, and bundle impact before acceptance.
Automated and manual evidence records viewport, keyboard, focus order, reduced-motion/status update handling, and major known limits without claiming final M7/M8 accessibility completion.
Accessibility fixes do not introduce client-side authoritative state, hidden network calls, telemetry, accounts, or Electron IPC changes.
required_tests: Accessibility evidence includes keyboard-only smoke, focus-order checks, viewport overflow checks, non-color status checks, and screen-reader label spot checks for core flows.
notes: This is core-flow Alpha accessibility, not final localization or platform matrix completion.
Do not add telemetry or account features to collect accessibility evidence.
If a new accessibility dependency is necessary, route dependency justification before implementation.
next_action: Lead accept M6 accessibility after QA ACCEPT and metadata reconciliation, then run integration gates.
accessibility_evidence: Keyboard focus order smoke covers map mode controls, zoom controls, map viewport focus, scenario start, preview, confirm, save, and load.

Map viewport exposes a focusable read-model region with arrow/Home/End district navigation, visible focus, aria-keyshortcuts, aria-roledescription, and a polite selected-district status; it only routes through the existing client selection callback and does not write authoritative WorldState.
M6 Alpha command and save statuses are persistent polite live regions with data-status-kind text/state attributes and aria-describedby descriptions, not color-only indicators.

M6 scenario selection, command preview/confirm, save/load checkpoint, policy/event choices, resolved events, encyclopedia content tags, terminal conditions, adviser candidates, and reason codes expose text/list semantics and screen-reader descriptions.
Viewport checks cover 1280x720, 1280x800, 1920x1080, and 2560x1080 with a 20px root font-size spot check on the M6 overflow test.
Reduced-motion smoke emulates prefers-reduced-motion: reduce and checks the CSS transition duration clamp.
known_limits: This is Alpha core-flow accessibility hardening, not final M7/M8 accessibility completion or a localization/platform accessibility matrix.

No new .storybook stories were added because .storybook/** was not in the allowed path list for this route; existing Storybook build still passes, while M6 normal/empty/error/extreme coverage is in Vitest and Playwright.
Manual screen-reader verification is represented by automated role/name/live-region spot checks and recorded evidence; no claim of final assistive-technology certification is made.

Playwright establishes the initial focus-order test from the first map mode control because Chromium did not consistently move focus from the browser/document start with the first Tab in this environment.
writer_replacement_reason: original writer closed after repeated wait timeouts and interrupt收口指令 produced no handoff; transient allowed-path diff observed before close was not committed and is not treated as authoritative
